fun_fact: The largest living organism on Earth isn't a blue whale or a sequoia — it's a honey fungus (Armillaria ostoyae) in Oregon spanning 9.6 km². But fungi's real superpower is underground: their vast mycelial networks quietly partner with plant roots, shuttling nutrients and water across forests. These networks store an estimated 36% of annual global carbon emissions in soil - making them one of nature's most underrated climate heroes. See this video
